onboarding.subtitle: We will get SilkScribe ready for your first transcription.

parakeet-tdt-0.6b-v2.name: Parakeet V2
parakeet-tdt-0.6b-v2.description: English only. The best model for English speakers.

moonshine-base.name: Moonshine Base
moonshine-base.description: Very fast, English only. Handles accents well.

moonshine-small-streaming-en.name: Moonshine V2 Small
moonshine-small-streaming-en.description: Fast, English only. Good balance of speed and accuracy.

breeze-asr.name: Breeze ASR
breeze-asr.description: Optimized for Taiwanese Mandarin. Code-switching support.

sense-voice-int8.name: SenseVoice
sense-voice-int8.description: Very fast. Chinese, English, Japanese, Korean, Cantonese.

microphone.title: Allow microphone access
microphone.description: SilkScribe needs your mic to turn speech into text.
microphone.whyItMattersBody: SilkScribe only listens while you record. Without mic access, it has nothing to transcribe.
microphone.breaksWithoutBody: Recording can start, but SilkScribe cannot hear your voice.
microphone.afterGrantBody: SilkScribe can record your voice and continue setup.

steps.step3: If no prompt appears, open System Settings > Privacy & Security > Microphone.
steps.step4: Turn on SilkScribe, then return here and check again.

microphone.manualHint: If you chose “Don’t Allow,” turn SilkScribe on in System Settings > Privacy & Security > Microphone.

statusHelp.checking: Checking whether microphone access is already on.
statusHelp.error: SilkScribe could not verify microphone access.
statusHelp.requesting: The macOS permission prompt should appear now.
statusHelp.waiting_for_user: Allow the permission, then come back and check again.

accessibility.title: Allow SilkScribe to type for you
accessibility.description: SilkScribe needs Accessibility access to place text into your apps.
accessibility.whyItMattersBody: SilkScribe puts finished text into the field you’re using. macOS protects that with Accessibility access.
accessibility.breaksWithoutBody: SilkScribe may transcribe, but it cannot reliably type the result into your apps.
accessibility.afterGrantBody: SilkScribe can type into apps and finish setup normally.

steps.step2: If macOS sends you to System Settings, continue there.
steps.step3: Open Privacy & Security > Accessibility and find SilkScribe.
steps.step4: Turn on SilkScribe, then return here and check again.

accessibility.manualHint: If SilkScribe still looks disabled, reopen the app after turning it on, then check again.

statusHelp.checking: Checking whether Accessibility access is already on.
statusHelp.error: SilkScribe could not verify Accessibility access.
statusHelp.requesting: The macOS approval flow should open now.
statusHelp.waiting_for_user: Turn SilkScribe on in System Settings > Privacy & Security > Accessibility, then check again.

setup.title: SilkScribe is getting ready.
setup.description: We’re preparing the best offline speech engine for {{language}}, then you’ll try a quick test.
setup.engineBody: SilkScribe is choosing the best fit for {{language}} and setting it up locally.
setup.shortcutBody: Hold {{shortcut}} to speak. Release it to transcribe.
setup.prepTitle: This should stay short and feel automatic
setup.prepBody: While setup runs, SilkScribe is downloading or loading the speech engine it picked for this machine. You can just wait here for the next step.
setup.ready: Everything is ready. Opening the practice step now.

panelHelp.resolving: SilkScribe is checking what’s available and choosing the best engine for this device.
panelHelp.downloading: The required files are downloading now.
panelHelp.preparing: The download is done. SilkScribe is loading everything locally.
panelHelp.ready: Setup is done. Next: one quick real test.

errors.loadModels: SilkScribe couldn’t load the available speech engines.
errors.selectModel: SilkScribe couldn’t prepare your speech engine.

shortcutHabit.title: One shortcut, one quick habit
shortcutHabit.description: SilkScribe is built around a very simple rhythm: hold, speak, release.
shortcutHabit.point2: Release it when you are done talking.
shortcutHabit.point3: SilkScribe turns that short burst into text right away.

focusField.title: Always focus the place you want text to go
focusField.description: SilkScribe sends the result into the field that is active when transcription finishes.
focusField.point1: Click into the text box you want before you start talking.
focusField.point2: Keep that field focused until the transcription finishes.
focusField.point3: During onboarding, we provide a practice box for this exact reason.

shortBursts.title: Short bursts feel fastest
shortBursts.description: New users usually get the cleanest first results when they keep each recording brief.
shortBursts.point1: Try one or two sentences at a time.
shortBursts.point3: You can always record another burst immediately after.

quietSpace.title: A quieter space makes the first win easier
quietSpace.description: SilkScribe works best when your voice stands out clearly from the room around you.
quietSpace.point1: Use the microphone you normally prefer if you have one.
quietSpace.point2: Keep fans, speakers, or background chatter as low as you can.
quietSpace.point3: Speak at a steady, normal pace.

privacy.title: Your setup stays on the essentials
privacy.description: This onboarding avoids extra configuration so you can get to a working first transcription faster.
privacy.point1: Permissions come first because they are critical to the app working correctly.
privacy.point2: The speech engine is chosen automatically in the background.
privacy.point3: You can fine-tune settings later after you have the core workflow working.

firstWin.title: The next screen is not a fake demo
firstWin.description: You are about to try a real push-to-talk transcription inside the actual app.
firstWin.point2: Use your normal shortcut exactly the way you will use it later.
firstWin.point3: If text appears in the box, SilkScribe is ready for real work.

welcome.title: Set up SilkScribe in a minute.
welcome.description: We’ll turn on the permissions it needs, finish setup in the background, and let you try one real dictation.

permissions.title: Clear permission help
permissions.description: See exactly what to allow and why.

engine.title: Automatic setup
engine.description: SilkScribe picks the right offline engine for this device.

practice.title: One real test
practice.description: Make sure everything works before you start.

welcome.outcome: Hold your shortcut, speak, and SilkScribe will place the text where you need it.

step2.title: Allow text access
step2.description: So SilkScribe can type into your apps.

step3.title: Try it once
step3.description: Setup finishes, then you run one quick test.

welcome.permissionPromise: These steps are required for SilkScribe to work properly.

practice.title: Try SilkScribe once
practice.description: Click the practice box, hold your shortcut, say a short sentence, then release.

step1.title: Focus the practice box
step1.description: Keep this box selected so SilkScribe knows where to place the text.

step2.title: Hold {{shortcut}} and speak
step2.description: Say one short sentence in a natural voice.

step3.title: Release to transcribe
step3.description: If the text appears here, SilkScribe is ready.

practice.tipBody: Say: “SilkScribe is ready for my notes today.”
practice.helper: Keep this box focused, then use your shortcut. Your words should appear here.
practice.placeholder: Hold your shortcut, speak, then release. Your text will appear here…
practice.readyToFinish: Your test worked. You can start using SilkScribe.
practice.prompt: You can skip this for now and test it later.
practice.finishFailed: SilkScribe couldn’t finish onboarding. Please try again.

modelSettings.title: {{model}} Settings
modelSettings.noSettingsNeeded: This model works automatically with no configuration needed.

general.pageDescription: Set the shortcut, choose your microphone, and keep the basics feeling automatic.

sections.recordingDescription: These are the controls most people touch first.
sections.feedbackDescription: Audio cues help you know when SilkScribe starts and stops listening.
sections.appDescription: Choose how the SilkScribe app itself should feel.

general.title: General

shortcut.title: Start recording
shortcut.description: Choose the shortcut you press to speak and transcribe.

transcribe.name: Transcribe Shortcut
transcribe.description: The keyboard shortcut to record and transcribe your voice.

cancel.name: Cancel Shortcut
cancel.description: The keyboard shortcut to cancel the current recording.

transcribe_with_post_process.name: Post-Processing Hotkey
transcribe_with_post_process.description: Optional: A dedicated hotkey that always applies AI post-processing to your transcription.

errors.reset: Failed to reset shortcut to original value

language.title: Language
language.description: Select the language for speech recognition. Auto will automatically determine the language, while selecting a specific language can improve accuracy for that language.
language.descriptionUnsupported: Parakeet model automatically detects the language. No manual selection is needed.

pushToTalk.label: Hold to record
pushToTalk.description: Press and hold your shortcut while you speak, then release to finish.

models.pageDescription: Pick the model you want SilkScribe to use, keep your installed models tidy, and download more when you need them.
models.title: Transcription Models
models.description: Select a transcription model or download additional models. Different models offer varying levels of accuracy and speed.
models.currentModelSettingsDescription: Only the active model’s language options stay visible here.
models.noActiveModelDescription: Download a model below and SilkScribe will use it for your next transcription.
models.noExtraInstalled: You only have your current model installed right now.
models.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete {{modelName}}? You will need to download it again to use it.
models.deleteActiveConfirm: {{modelName}} is your active model. Deleting it will stop transcriptions until you select a new model. Are you sure?

audioFeedback.label: Audio Feedback
audioFeedback.description: Play sound when recording starts and stops

outputDevice.title: Output Device
outputDevice.description: Select your preferred audio output device for feedback sounds

volume.title: Volume
volume.description: Adjust the volume of audio feedback sounds

advanced.pageDescription: Most people can leave these alone. Use them when you need SilkScribe to behave differently on your machine.
advanced.title: Advanced

groupDescriptions.app: Startup and overlay behavior for the app itself.
groupDescriptions.output: How finished text gets placed into the app you are using.
groupDescriptions.transcription: Small adjustments that change how SilkScribe shapes your text.
groupDescriptions.history: Retention and cleanup settings for transcripts and recordings.
groupDescriptions.support: Maintenance tools and folders that help with troubleshooting.
groupDescriptions.experimental: Features that are still evolving and may change.

experimentalToggle.label: Experimental Features
experimentalToggle.description: Turn on features that are still being tested.

startHidden.label: Start Hidden
startHidden.description: Open SilkScribe in the background instead of showing the app window.

autostart.label: Launch on Startup
autostart.description: Start SilkScribe automatically when you sign in to your computer.

showTrayIcon.label: Show Tray Icon
showTrayIcon.description: Keep SilkScribe available from your menu bar or system tray.

overlay.title: Overlay position
overlay.description: Show a small status overlay while SilkScribe is recording or transcribing. On Linux, None is usually the safest choice.

pasteMethod.title: How text is inserted
pasteMethod.description: Choose whether SilkScribe types text directly, uses a paste shortcut, or only copies the result without inserting it.

typingTool.title: Linux typing tool
typingTool.description: Pick the Linux tool SilkScribe should use when it inserts text directly. Auto chooses the best available option.

clipboardHandling.title: Clipboard behavior
clipboardHandling.description: Choose whether SilkScribe should keep your existing clipboard untouched or leave the transcription in it after insertion.
clipboardHandling.directInsertHint: SilkScribe will insert text directly here so your clipboard stays unchanged.

autoSubmit.title: Send after insertion
autoSubmit.description: Optionally press one more key after SilkScribe inserts the text, such as Enter to send a message.

translateToEnglish.label: Translate to English
translateToEnglish.description: Automatically translate speech from other languages to English during transcription.
translateToEnglish.descriptionUnsupported: Translation is not supported by the {{model}} model.

modelUnload.title: Free memory when idle
modelUnload.description: Unload the current model after it sits unused so SilkScribe gives memory back to the system.

customWords.title: Custom Words
customWords.description: Add names, products, or terms SilkScribe should try harder to get right.

appleIntelligence.title: Apple Intelligence
appleIntelligence.description: Runs fully on-device. No API key or network access is required.
appleIntelligence.requirements: Requires an Apple Silicon Mac running macOS Tahoe (26.0) or later. Apple Intelligence must be enabled in System Settings.
appleIntelligence.unavailable: Apple Intelligence is not available on this device. Requires an Apple Silicon Mac running macOS Tahoe (26.0) or later with Apple Intelligence enabled in System Settings.

baseUrl.title: Base URL
baseUrl.description: API base URL for the selected provider. Only the custom provider can be edited.

apiKey.title: API Key
apiKey.description: API key for the selected provider.

model.title: Model
model.descriptionApple: Provide an optional numeric token limit or keep the default on-device preset.
model.descriptionCustom: Provide the model identifier expected by your custom endpoint.
model.descriptionDefault: Choose a model exposed by the selected provider.

prompts.title: Prompt

selectedPrompt.title: Selected Prompt
selectedPrompt.description: Select a template for refining transcriptions or create a new one. Use ${output} inside the prompt text to reference the captured transcript.

prompts.promptInstructionsPlaceholder: Write the instructions to run after transcription. Example: Improve grammar and clarity for the following text: ${output}
prompts.promptTip: Tip: Use ${output} to insert the transcribed text in your prompt.
prompts.selectToEdit: Select a prompt above to view and edit its details.
prompts.createFirst: Click 'Create New Prompt' above to create your first post-processing prompt.

history.pageDescription: Browse what SilkScribe captured, replay audio, and save anything you want to keep close.
history.title: History
history.empty: No transcriptions yet. Start recording to build your history!
history.emptyDescription: Your saved transcripts and recordings will appear here after your first dictation.
history.deleteError: Failed to delete entry. Please try again.
history.loadError: Unable to load history right now. Please try again.

logDirectory.title: Log Directory
logDirectory.description: Location where log files are stored

updateChecks.label: Check for Updates
updateChecks.description: Automatically check for new versions of SilkScribe

soundTheme.label: Sound Theme
soundTheme.description: Choose a sound theme for recording start and stop feedback

historyLimit.title: History Limit
historyLimit.description: Maximum number of history entries to keep

recordingRetention.title: Auto-Delete Recordings
recordingRetention.description: Automatically delete old recordings to save space

alwaysOnMicrophone.label: Always-On Microphone
alwaysOnMicrophone.description: Keep microphone active for faster response

clamshellMicrophone.title: Clamshell Microphone
clamshellMicrophone.description: Microphone to use when laptop lid is closed

postProcessingToggle.label: Post Processing
postProcessingToggle.description: Enable AI-powered text refinement after transcription

appendTrailingSpace.label: Append Trailing Space
appendTrailingSpace.description: Add a space after pasted transcription

keyboardImplementation.title: Keyboard Implementation
keyboardImplementation.bindingsReset: Keyboard shortcuts were incompatible and reset to defaults

pasteDelay.title: Paste Delay
pasteDelay.description: Delay before sending the paste keystroke (in milliseconds) when SilkScribe is using a clipboard hotkey.

appDataDirectory.title: App Data Directory
appDataDirectory.description: Location where SilkScribe stores its data

accessibility.permissionsDescription: SilkScribe needs accessibility permissions to type transcribed text.

appLanguage.title: Application Language
appLanguage.description: Change the language of the SilkScribe interface

overlay.listeningDetail: Speak naturally and SilkScribe will capture the audio.
overlay.errorDetail: Try again or cancel the current pass.

home.title: Welcome back
home.subtitle: Your recent dictation activity and listening history.

welcome.title: Welcome back.
welcome.readyDescription: Everything looks ready. Hold your shortcut, speak naturally, and SilkScribe will place the text where you need it.
welcome.setupDescription: Finish the essentials once, then SilkScribe stays out of your way and is ready whenever you need quick dictation.

setup.title: A few essentials still need attention.
setup.description: Once your shortcut, model, and permissions are in place, SilkScribe is ready for everyday dictation.

permissions.label: Permissions
permissions.ready: Ready to type into your apps
permissions.needsAttention: Needs attention before dictation is complete

firstUse.title: Your first transcript will show up here.
firstUse.description: Click into a text field, hold your shortcut, speak a sentence, then release. SilkScribe will save the result here so you can revisit it later.

empty.title: No recent transcriptions yet.
empty.description: Once you record something, SilkScribe will keep the transcript and audio here automatically.

error.title: Couldn't load your dashboard.
error.description: Try again to refresh your recent activity.
